patch.value: pm deps context format reports missing_count without enumerating missing references and disagrees with tree format

pm deps --format context returns a top-level missing_count scalar (3 on pm-96h7) but the payload contains no list of which references are missing or which neighbor declares them; pm deps --format tree on the same item reports missing_count 0. Agents cannot distinguish ignorable legacy ghosts on closed neighbors from repairable typos on active items, and the two formats contradict each other.

context and tree formats agree on missing_count semantics for the same item and depth, or the difference is explicitly documented in the payload; the context payload enumerates missing references with the declaring node id, the dangling target id, and edge kind, bounded by the existing node/edge limits; missing references are classified active vs legacy-terminal following the pm-2ler split so agents know what is repairable; JSON and TOON shapes stay in sync with cli-contracts and the MCP schema; covering spec added

value.note: Owns missing_count computation for deps payloads
Bounded context traversal; has no missing-reference handling today

deps command wiring for tree/graph/context formats

value.note: Bounded context traversal; has no missing-reference handling today

patch.value: Evidence (2026-07-16, v2026.7.16 on main): `pm deps pm-96h7 --format context` returns missing_count:3 with node_count:21/edge_count:12, meta shows visitedNodes:737/inspectedEdges:1223 — but no missing-reference rows anywhere in the payload; `pm deps pm-96h7 --json` (tree) returns missing_count:0 and missing:null for the same root. No CLI flag enumerates the refs (--help audited). Validate's dependency_references check is status:ok with active=0, so the 3 are almost certainly legacy-terminal ghosts encountered on closed depth-2 neighbors (pm-2ler class) — exactly why enumeration + classification matters: an agent seeing a bare count cannot tell repairable active typos from ignorable history. Sibling precedent: pm-p9sc (GH-535, CLOSED PR#545) fixed the inverse contradiction (deps under-reporting vs validate). Fix should surface bounded missing rows (declaring node, dangling target, kind, active|legacy classification) in context format and align tree-format semantics.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Duplicate check: searched all-status for 'deps context missing count enumerate', 'missing_count', 'graph context missing references' — only pm-p9sc (closed, inverse defect), pm-8xr8 (consumer story), pm-2ler (closed classification precedent). No open owner; registry consulted.

value.note: documented missing_count traversal scope and tree-format agreement

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Fix evidence 2026-07-17: context format missing_count was workspace-global (counted every dangling target in the tracker) while tree counted traversal-encountered missing nodes - the pm-96h7 3-vs-0 contradiction. Now missing_count is traversal-scoped (missing nodes reachable under the same direction/kinds/max-depth that built the packet, independent of node/token pagination so it is stable across cursor pages), missing_scope:"traversal" documents the semantics in the payload, missing_reference_count totals the dangling declarations inside the packet, and missing_references enumerates bounded rows (holder_id, target_id, kind, source, holder_status, legacy_terminal) reusing the pm-2ler active/legacy split so agents can tell repairable typos from historical debt. no-active-blocker sentinels are excluded. Tree agreement verified in spec and in a packed-tarball E2E (tree missing:1 == context missing_count:1 on the same root/depth). Covering specs: deps-command.spec.ts (enumeration, bounding via --edge-limit, direction scoping, legacy classification on closed holders, sentinel skip via raw legacy fixture).

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: When two output formats disagree on a count, scope the count to the traversal that produced the payload and stamp the scope into the payload (missing_scope) - agreement claims in docs alone rot; a self-describing field survives format evolution. Computing the missing set from an unbounded-closure over the same direction/kinds/depth (ignoring node/token pagination) keeps the count stable across cursor pages.

patch.value: Context and tree now use traversal-scoped missing semantics; context enumerates bounded holder/target/kind/source/status rows with legacy-terminal classification, sentinel exclusion, and SDK/CLI/MCP/contract parity.
Delivered traversal-scoped missing-reference parity and bounded classification with exact coverage, contract parity, and installed-package proof.
